rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JACQUELINE WARD 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the co-trustees' attorneys' fees requested are reasonable. Many billing entries reflect time spent to address issues that were raised in prior accounting periods. Some of these issues were addressed again this time, such as clearly identifying the payees, especially where one of the fiduciaries was reimbursed, and not setting forth all vested or contingent beneficiaries of the trust, as required by CRC 7.902. Furthermore, many of the services provided by the attorney, such as reminding the co-trustees of upcoming deadlines, providing courtesy copies to the Court, drafting initial pleadings and proposed orders, and communicating with the bonding company, etc., could be done by a paralegal. NB: A fiduciary is required to sign and verify his/her own pleadings; time spent to prepare an attorney declaration to provide information missing from a petition is not reasonable. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL L BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. Copies of responsive papers were received on 4/3/23, after the 3/30/23 deadline. Nonetheless, they were reviewed in hopes of not having to continue the hearing again. 2. The records indicate that the examiners who were assigned the two ex parte petitions filed on 11/17/21 and the one filed on 9/14/22 never received an appropriate response to issues they raised. Those ex parte petitions are no longer pending. They must be filed again. Alternatively, the administrator might wish to file written consents from the other heirs to her payment of additional fees to her attorneys for the litigation. 3. An appearance might be required after these remaining issues are addressed for the issue of the late filed election. DEADLINE for providing endorsed filed courtesy copies to the assigned Examiner is 5/24/23. Please be aware that court clerks need time to process mail, etc. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. A supplement or amendment to a pleading should stand on its own and not refer to an examiner's notes or email, etc., which are not otherwise part of the record. A supplement does not require notice. [Probate Code Section 1205 and CRC 7.53(b).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHERINE PATRICIA PRONGOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] 2. There was no Attachment 1 to the I&A filed on 11/23/22. 3. If an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. 4. A petition for approval of an account or a report accompanying the petition shall contain all the statements required by Probate Code Section 1064(a). Did either fiduciary have a family or affiliate relationship with any agent he or she hired during administration? How was cash invested? 5. It appears that cash was not invested in an interest-bearing account, as required by Probate Code Section 9652, as no interest was reported in the accounting. 6. Is all cash in excess of the FDIC insured amount being held in one bank account? [Probate Code Section 9700.] 7. The detailed schedules supporting the summary of account must show the nature or purpose of each receipt or disbursement, including the name of the payee, and the date thereof. [Probate Code Section 1062.] In other words, the four columns should show the date, the source or payee, the description of the receipt or disbursement, and the amount. 8. The filing fee for this petition was $435, not $450. Endorsed filed courtesy copy of a proof of service of a complete Notice of Hearing must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KIMBERLY ANNE PUCKETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the pleading filed on 3/22/23 was not a fully amended petition but merely an amendment to the petition. [CRC 7.3.] Notwithstanding Executor's allegation in paragraph 8 of the "amended first and final report" that the daughter is entitled to notice, no proof of service of notice to her was filed. The executor, not the attorney, should have changed the prayer in paragraph 4 of the "amended first and final report". NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. (An unfiled order IS a proposed order until it is signed. There is no need to put "proposed" in the caption.) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALICE M. WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. NB: Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the  net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF QUIJUAN SPENCER MALOOF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No original will has been lodged. (2) The copy of the will attached to the petition was not properly executed (no witness attestations). (3) Under what authority can the Court admit a will that is not properly executed? (4) If the petitioner is the trustee of the devisee trust, all beneficiaries of the trust must be identified and must receive notice. (5) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (6) The relationship to the decedent of a person listed in item 8 of a petition seeking appointment is not their [nominated] fiduciary capacity but either a familial relation or stranger. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BEVERLY WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) Contestants to report the court if PC 8250(a) has been met, including but not limited to service of Summons on all persons entitled. (2) Counsel for competing petitioners (Mr. Whitfield and Ms. Maximo) must cure the following procedural issues: (A) All persons named in the purported Will must be served with Notice of Petition to Administer Estate [DE-121] as the court instructed at the last hearing. (B) Need Duties and Liabilities of each proposed administrator. (C) Need Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number from each proposed administrator. (D) Need Clarification of Bond amount. Based on the Petition #3.d, $487,000 appears appropriate. (E)Petition to supplement Carolyn West's address on Attachment #8. No address listed. (F)Need Proposed Order. parties appearing should check in for the hearing with their full name. cOUNSEL AND PARTIES MAY APPEAR PERSONALLY OR REMOTELY THROUGH zOOM OR cOURTcALL. fOR A VIDEO APPEARANCE, GO TO ZOOM.US, CLICK "JOIN A MEETING" AND INPUT MEETING id 160 225 4765 AND PASSWORD 514879. fOR AN AUDIO-ONLY APPEARANCE THROUGH zOOM, CALL 1-669-254-5252 AND KEY IN MEETING id "160 225 4765#", THEN PARTICIPANT id "0#", THEN PASSWORD "514879#". (tOLL RATES MAY APPLY).) tHE cOURT PREFERS THAT COUNSEL AND PARTIES APPEARING BY VIDEO INPUT THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME INTO THE "yOUR nAME" DIALOGUE BOX. cOUNSEL AND PARTIES MAY APPEAR BY AUDIO-ONLY TELEPHONE THROUGH cOURTcALL BY CALLING 1-888-882-6878. cOUNSEL OR PARTIES APPEARING IN PERSON MUST WEAR A MASK COVERING THEIR NOSE AND MOUTH AT ALL TIMES WHILE IN THE COURTHOUSE. pLEASE REVIEW lrsf 14.1(d) FOR MORE INFORMATION.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GARY Y. TAKEOKA REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Reform Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please see the procedural issues as below: Petition: 1. A copy of the Trust subject to reform should be provided. 2. Clarification of the relief. Prayer appears broad and ambiguous as it does not set forth which provision needs to be reformed and how. 3. Notice of Hearing and a copy of petition to be directly served on all persons who are entitled pursuant to Prob. Code 17203 and CRC 7.51. The examiner is unable to review if the notice requirement has been completely met as to all persons who served in c/o. 4. Proposed Order to be submitted. LRSF14.7.A. Objection: 1. Proof of service of opposition to meet CRC 7.51. The examiner is unable to review if the service has been completely met as to all persons who served in c/o. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD S. ARMSTRONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 5/7/2024. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 5/7/24. Appearance will be required on 5/7/24 if no petition has been filed. No SASE was provided; copies of signed orders cannot be returned to Counsel. NB: Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Since the petitioner is the sole heir and interested party and, therefore, did not need to give notice to herself, this defect was waived for this hearing.
